examples: Aortitis is the pathological term for inflammation of the aortic wall.
A report of Brech describes a badly diseased heart, enlarged to the left with a protruding aortic knob.
Aortic stenosis is a nasty disease.
An ultrasound, however, turned up a more serious condition: an abdominal aortic aneurysm (AAA).
Investigators studied a technique to replace the aortic valve that did not require surgery.
